title: Fetch latest GitHub release metadata
action: Add a new src/update/github.rs submodule (declared via `mod github;` in src/update/mod.rs) with an async function that fetches the latest release for vantt/herdr-go from the GitHub Releases REST API (GET https://api.github.com/repos/vantt/herdr-go/releases/latest, matching D1's 'releases/latest' channel) using the existing reqwest client already depended on (Cargo.toml) and parses the JSON response for the release's tag_name. Only fetch the tag name in this cell -- do not build asset-list parsing yet, that belongs to EP3 (checksum-gated download), which is out of scope here. Testability note: this repo has NO existing async-transport-injection precedent (the closure-injection pattern in src/doctor/checks.rs's offer_service_restart_with is synchronous, and the reqwest usage in src/main.rs around line 299 is a bare, non-injected client construction) -- do not try to force-fit either as-is. Recommended shape: split the network call from the parse step -- write a pure, synchronous function that takes the raw JSON response body as a String and returns the parsed tag_name (or a clear error on malformed JSON/missing field), and unit-test THAT function directly with fixture strings (no network, no injection machinery needed). Keep the actual `reqwest::get(...).await` call as a thin, deliberately-untested wrapper around the pure parser -- this satisfies 'no real network call in tests' without needing new async-closure generics. Wire this fetch function's result through self-update-merge-config-3's version-compare logic to produce the overall 'is an update available' decision (D2), but do not wire a CLI verb yet (EP6's job).

truths: Given a fixture JSON string shaped like a real GitHub release payload, the pure parse function correctly extracts tag_name
A malformed JSON string or one missing tag_name returns a clear error value from the pure parse function, never a panic

artifacts.substantive: exports a pure, synchronously-testable JSON-body parser plus a thin untested async reqwest wrapper around it; unit tests exercise only the parser, no real network call

key_links: src/update/mod.rs declares `mod github;` and composes github's fetch result with self-update-merge-config-3's version-compare function
Uses the existing reqwest dependency already in Cargo.toml -- no new HTTP crate added
prohibitions: No real network call inside unit tests
No asset-list/checksum parsing in this cell (EP3's scope)

verify: test -f src/update/github.rs && grep -q '#\[test\]' src/update/github.rs && grep -q 'mod github' src/update/mod.rs && cargo test --quiet update

trace.outcome: Added src/update/github.rs (pure parse_tag_name + thin async fetch_latest_tag + check_for_update composing fetch with version-compare, D1/D2); declared pub mod github in mod.rs. 10 update tests pass; clippy --lib clean.
trace.verify_output: 10 tests passed; 0 failed (6 existing compare tests + 4 new github parser tests: parses_tag_name_from_real_payload, malformed_json_returns_error_not_panic, missing_tag_name_returns_clear_error, empty_tag_name_returns_clear_error). File/grep guards all passed.
trace.verify_command: test -f src/update/github.rs && grep -q '#\[test\]' src/update/github.rs && grep -q 'mod github' src/update/mod.rs && cargo test --quiet update
